paper_title: Redundancy and Variation Partitioning Analysis in Diatom Community Composition

methods.description: This method uses redundancy analysis (RDA) with forward selection and Monte Carlo permutation tests to determine which geographical and environmental variables significantly explain variation in diatom community composition across lakes. Variation partitioning analysis is applied to assess the unique contributions of local environmental, climatic, spatial, and historical variables.

steps.description: Select a subset of lakes (n = 213) with available measurements of specific conductance, pH, Na+, K+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Cl−, NO3−-N, NH4+-N, PO43+-P, temperature, and spatial and historical variables.
steps.searchable_summary: filter lakes with complete environmental and spatial data

steps.description: Transform environmental variables logarithmically, except pH, which is square root transformed.
steps.searchable_summary: apply logarithmic and square root transformations to environmental variables

steps.description: Create three matrices: 1) biotic matrix with Hellinger-transformed presence-absence data, 2) matrix with limnological and temperature variables, 3) matrix with spatial and historical variables.
steps.searchable_summary: construct biotic, limnological, and spatial matrices

steps.description: Perform principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) to obtain PCNM vectors from geographic distances.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct PCoA to derive PCNM vectors

steps.description: Select significantly spatially autocorrelated PCNM vectors using Moran's I.
steps.searchable_summary: select significant PCNM vectors using Moran's I

steps.description: Model latitude and longitude using distance-based Moran Eigenvector Maps (dbMEM).
Perform variation inflation factor (VIF) analysis to reduce multicollinearity, removing variables with VIF > 10.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct VIF analysis to remove multicollinear variables

steps.description: Conduct variation partitioning to assess unique contributions of environmental, climatic, spatial, and historical variables.
Use Monte Carlo permutation tests (999 permutations) to assess significance of ordination axes.
steps.searchable_summary: validate ordination axes with Monte Carlo permutation tests

steps.description: Adjust multiple coefficients of determination (R² adj) to correct for sample size and number of predictors.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust R² for sample size and predictor count
